calendar.matters:
Motion To File Document Under Seal; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar May-07-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Lloyd Bookman, Esq. Alicia Macklin, Esq. 415-8758500 Hoper Lundy & Bookman for Petitioner
David Houska, Esq. Deputy Attorney General 510-219-6393 CA Dept. of Justice for Respondent (In-person)

Parties state appearance. The Court announces the tentative ruling. The Court states a timely notice of contest the TR was received.

ARGUED.
Mr. Bookman contests and presents arguments; contains pricing information, public disclosure, anti-trust risks, redact pricing options, 8 hospital organizations, orally agrees to removal of unredacted/redacted records (now).
Court comments and poses questions; anti-trust argument not persuasive, limit it, change approach to harm issue, timing, states everything lodged is removed, Petitioner has until 6/1/26 to file a second amended petition.
Mr. Houska has no objection to an amended writ, objects to additions.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling with modification.

Petitioner California Hospital Association's Motion To File Document Under Seal is DENIED.

(see word minutes for full details)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for full details and modification)

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Motion To File Document Under Seal; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Apr-29-2026 Continued To May-07-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER CALIFORNIA HOSPITAL ASSOCIATION Motion To File Document Under Seal.

Continued on the court's own motion to be heard on May 7, 2026.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

DEMURRER to PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Feb-17-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Lloyd Bookman Esq. Alicia Macklin Esq. 310-551-8185 Hooper Lundy & Bookman for Petitioner.
David Houska, Esq. Sofia Tonna Esq. Deputy Attorney General 510-219-6393 for Respondent.
Court reporter: Valerie Nunemacher, CSR #10783, 208-660-5126, valeriecsr@aol.com (Zoom)

Parties state appearance. The Court announces the tentative ruling. The Court states a timely notice of contest the TR was received.

ARGUED.
Mr. Hoska submits to the TR. Mr. Bookman contests and presents arguments; requests more time to amend; raises 3 points; review of agency's action; cost standards; cost targets; standing; cites supreme court cases, uncertain future.
Court comments and poses questions.
Mr. Hoska responds to arguments; difference in opinion, lawfulness of cost targets, don't know what injury will be, does not oppose 30 days to amend.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Respondents Office of Health Care Affordability, Department of Health Access and Information, Health Care Accessibility Board and Elizabeth Landsberg's (collectively, "OHCA") demurrer to Petitioner California Hospital Association's petition for writ of mandate.

Taken Under Submission.***

(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court reporter: Valerie Nunemacher, CSR #10783, 208-660-5126, valeriecsr@aol.comReported. (302/JMQ)

***After the hearing, the court signs an order. (see scanned order for details)***
